summary.short_rationale: Glebkudr adds a strong deep-practitioner context-engineering and deterministic agent workflow angle. The deterministic report now sees all 4 repaired matrix_export cells.
summary.strongest_argument_for: The passport shows signature value around context compression, repository semantic packaging, deterministic multi-agent pipelines, and cost-aware model/tool evaluation for coding systems.
summary.strongest_argument_against: The accepted matrix already has dense agent_ops/design_agentic_dev_workflow coverage, so broad agentic workflow routing could duplicate existing experts.

good_fit: token cost and latency optimization through context pruning
frontier model comparison for concrete coding and OCR tasks
anti-hype critique of naive vibe coding on large codebases
poor_fit: neutral tool comparisons where a highly opinionated source would distort balance

taxonomy_recommendation.rationale: Context compression is a recurring AI-assisted development problem distinct from generic prompt formatting and generic inference cost/latency.

risk_notes: Strong personal/tool-builder bias may over-route his own methods.
Opinionated anti-hype is useful but can reduce balance.
The repaired deterministic report is overlap-heavy; route Glebkudr narrowly for context engineering and model-comparison depth.
recommended_followup: Passport comparison hardening has been implemented: matrix_export_incomplete is surfaced and strong/signature matrix_cells are repaired into matrix_export.
Keep the taxonomy decision for prompt_engineering/context_compression.
